subject: [PATCH v3 05/16] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

2026-05-23.patch_summary: This patch adds two new flags to the change_protection() function in the Linux kernel: MM_CP_UFFD_RWP and MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE. These flags are used for userfaultfd (UFFD) read-write protection (RWP), which allows userspace to specify ranges of memory that should be protected from write access, but still allow reads. The patch also updates various kernel functions to handle these new flags correctly, ensuring that UFFD RWP is properly supported across different types of memory mappings.
